_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to organize logging and auditing procedures including server-based logging
reference_document_element_description: Monitor organizational compliance with data protection obligations, conduct internal audits of processing activities, maintain the register of processing activities, and report compliance posture to senior management and supervisory authorities as required.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-T-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-T-007 (OP, cosine: 0.434), OP-T-002 (OP, cosine: 0.434).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.0/10 (raw 0.499). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [ability, auditing, based, logging, organize, procedures] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, audits, authorities, compliance, conduct, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.862 (P=0.878, R=0.847). P exceeds R by 0.031 — the DCWF element's 10 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 31 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Ability to organize logging and auditing procedures including server-based loggi" and "Monitor organizational compliance with data protection obligations, conduct inte" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.440 < STS 0.499 (gap -0.060). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [ability, auditing, based] and [activities, audits, authorities] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.9%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [ability, auditing, based, logging, organize, procedures]. WIDAI terms: [activities, audits, authorities, compliance, conduct, data].
